get.description: Returns all active users from the in-memory cache for the specified tenant within the given time range. Each user object includes identity, browser, device, platform, country, language, and role information.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

get.description: Returns the total count of distinct active users recorded within the given time range for the specified tenant. Useful as a headline KPI on dashboards.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

get.description: Returns the count of users whose first recorded activity falls at or after the `start` boundary, i.e. users who are new within the queried window. Useful for tracking user acquisition over time.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

get.description: Returns the most common browser language codes among active users within the given time range, sorted by frequency descending. Each entry contains a `value` (language code) and a `count`.

parameters.name: tenantId

get.description: Returns the most common operating system platforms (e.g. Windows, macOS, Linux) among active users within the given time range, sorted by frequency descending. Each entry contains a `value` (platform name) and a `count`.

get.description: Returns the most common web browsers among active users within the given time range, sorted by frequency descending. Each entry contains a `value` (browser name) and a `count`.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

get.description: Extracts and counts the email domain portion of each active user's identifier within the given time range, returning the most common domains sorted by frequency descending. Useful for understanding which organisations are most active on the platform.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

get.description: Queries the custom-events cache for the given tenant and time range, then groups events by their `name` field and counts occurrences. Returns an array of `{ value, count }` pairs sorted by count descending — useful for a ranked event-type breakdown.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

get.description: Filters the custom-events cache to events matching the provided `eventName` query parameter, then groups the results by `data.widgetName` and counts occurrences. Returns an array of `{ value, count }` pairs sorted by count descending — useful for per-widget interaction breakdowns.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

get.description: Returns all custom events from the cache within the given time range for the specified tenant. Each event contains `name`, `date`, and `data`. Set `withId=true` to also include `identifyId` and `sessionId` on each event object.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

parameters.name: withId
parameters.description: When true, includes identifyId and sessionId in each event object. Defaults to false.

get.description: Aggregates session-initialised events into time buckets using an automatically chosen resolution: ranges ≤ 1 hour are bucketed by minute, ranges ≤ 1 day by hour, and longer ranges by day. All bucket slots within the queried range are pre-populated with zero counts so charts render continuous lines. Returns an array of `{ time, count }` pairs sorted chronologically.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

get.description: Returns raw session-initialised events from the cache within the given time range for the specified tenant. Each entry contains `time` (ISO 8601), `eventId`, `identifyId`, `inferredLocation`, and `userType`.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

get.description: Aggregates page views from the cache by device ID for the given tenant and time range. URLs matching the pattern `/device/{numericId}` are grouped by ID and counted. Returns an array of `{ path, count }` pairs sorted by count descending.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

get.description: Aggregates page views by a specific URL path segment type: `group`, `device`, `reports`, or `dashboard`. URLs containing `/{type}/{numericId}` are extracted and grouped by their numeric ID. Returns an array of `{ path, count }` pairs sorted by count descending.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

get.description: Aggregates page views by normalised URL pattern for the given tenant and time range. All numeric segments in the URL path are replaced with `*` before grouping, producing a count per page type rather than per specific entity. Returns an array of `{ path, count }` pairs sorted by count descending.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

get.description: Supports optional server-side filtering via filtrex (https://github.com/cshaa/filtrex) and field projection. All string values in filter expressions must be double-quoted.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
filtrex filter expression applied to mapped user objects. Strings must be double-quoted.

**Examples**
- `country == "Germany"` — users from Germany
- `platform == "desktop" and browser == "Chrome"` — desktop Chrome users
- `device in ("mobile", "tablet")` — mobile and tablet users only
- `roles != "guest"` — exclude guest users
- `domain ~= "siemens"` — users whose email domain contains "siemens"

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
Comma-separated user fields to return. Valid values: id, roles, country, sessionId, language, platform, browser, device, domain. Omit to return all fields.

**Examples**
- `id,country,roles` — identity, location, and role breakdown
- `id,platform,browser,device` — device and browser profile only
- `id,domain,language` — domain and language breakdown

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Field and optional direction to sort results by, in the format `field` or `field:asc` / `field:desc`. Direction defaults to `asc` when omitted. Valid field values match those listed in `fields`.

**Examples**
- `country:asc` — alphabetical by country
- `domain:asc` — alphabetical by domain
- `language:asc` — alphabetical by language
- `platform:desc` — reverse-alphabetical by platform

200.description: Filtered and projected active user objects.

get.description: Supports optional server-side filtering via filtrex (https://github.com/cshaa/filtrex), field projection, and data sub-field projection. All string values in filter expressions must be double-quoted.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
filtrex filter expression applied to mapped event objects. Strings must be double-quoted.

**Examples**
- `name == "buttonClick"` — button click events only
- `name != "pageView"` — exclude page view events
- `name in ("buttonClick", "formSubmit")` — clicks and form submissions
- `identifyId == "user-001"` — events from a specific user

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
Comma-separated top-level event fields to return. Valid values: name, date, data, identifyId, sessionId. Omit to return all fields.

**Examples**
- `name,date,identifyId` — event name, timestamp, and user
- `name,data` — event name and raw payload only
- `name,date,sessionId` — event name, timestamp, and session context

parameters.name: dataFields
parameters.description:
Comma-separated dot-notation paths to project from event.data. Omit to return the full data object.

**Examples**
- `widgetName` — widget name only
- `widgetName,widgetId` — widget name and identifier
- `attributes.size,attributes.color` — specific nested attribute paths

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Field and optional direction to sort results by, in the format `field` or `field:asc` / `field:desc`. Direction defaults to `asc` when omitted. Valid field values match those listed in `fields`.

**Examples**
- `date:desc` — most recent events first
- `date:asc` — oldest events first
- `name:asc` — alphabetical by event name
- `identifyId:asc` — alphabetical by user

get.description: Pre-filtered to events whose name starts with "customEvent". The data field always conforms to CustomerCustomEventAttributes (action_type, category, label, metadata). Supports optional filtrex filtering (https://github.com/cshaa/filtrex) and data sub-field projection. All string values in filter expressions must be double-quoted.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
filtrex filter expression applied after the customEvent prefix pre-filter. Strings must be double-quoted.

**Examples**
- `name == "customEventButtonClick"` — specific customer event type
- `name in ("customEventButtonClick", "customEventFormSubmit")` — multiple customer event types
- `identifyId == "user-001"` — customer events from a specific user

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
Comma-separated top-level event fields to return. Valid values: name, date, data, identifyId, sessionId. Omit to return all fields.

**Examples**
- `name,date,identifyId` — event name, timestamp, and user
- `name,data` — event name and payload only
- `name,date,sessionId` — event name, timestamp, and session context

parameters.name: dataFields
parameters.description:
Comma-separated dot-notation paths to project from event.data. Valid root values: action_type, category, label, metadata. Omit to return the full data object.

**Examples**
- `category,label` — category and label only
- `action_type,category` — action type with category
- `label,metadata.size` — label with a specific metadata sub-field

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Field and optional direction to sort results by, in the format `field` or `field:asc` / `field:desc`. Direction defaults to `asc` when omitted. Valid field values match those listed in `fields`.

**Examples**
- `date:desc` — most recent events first
- `date:asc` — oldest events first
- `name:asc` — alphabetical by event name
- `identifyId:asc` — alphabetical by user

200.description: Filtered and projected customer custom event objects.

get.description:
Supports optional server-side filtering via filtrex (https://github.com/cshaa/filtrex), field projection, and sorting. All string values in filter expressions must be double-quoted. Use the `~=` operator for regex matching, e.g. to scope results to a specific group or device.

**Filter examples**
- All dashboards of group 123: `hash ~= "group/123/dashboard"`
- All dashboards of device 456: `hash ~= "device/456/dashboard"`
- Any dashboard across all groups or devices: `hash ~= "(group|device)/[0-9]+/dashboard"`
- Specific user type: `userType == "USER"`
- Multiple paths: `path in ("/home", "/settings")`

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
filtrex filter expression applied to mapped page-view objects. Strings must be double-quoted. Use `~=` for regex matching.

**Examples**
- `hash ~= "group/123/dashboard"` — all dashboards inside group 123
- `hash ~= "device/456/dashboard"` — all dashboards inside device 456
- `userType == "USER" and host == "app.example.com"` — authenticated users on a specific host
- `pageTitle ~= "Dashboard"` — all pages with "Dashboard" in the title
- `accountId == "acc-001"` — page views from a specific account

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
Comma-separated page-view fields to return. Valid values: id, identifyId, sessionId, date, scheme, host, path, queryString, hash, queryParams, remoteHost, referrer, screenHeight, screenWidth, languages, pageTitle, propertyKey, eventType, userType, accountId, globalContext. Omit to return all fields.

**Examples**
- `id,identifyId,hash,date` — minimal view with page path and user
- `id,pageTitle,accountId,userType` — page title per account and user type
- `id,hash,screenWidth,screenHeight` — page paths with screen resolution

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Field and optional direction to sort results by, in the format `field` or `field:asc` / `field:desc`. Direction defaults to `asc` when omitted. Valid field values match those listed in `fields`.

**Examples**
- `date:desc` — most recent events first
- `date:asc` — oldest events first
- `identifyId:asc` — alphabetical by user
- `pageTitle:asc` — alphabetical by page title

200.description: Filtered, sorted, and projected page-view objects.

get.description:
Filters page-views using a filtrex expression (same syntax as `/v2/pageViews`), then groups the matching records by the `groupBy` field and counts occurrences. Results are always returned sorted by `count` descending so the most-visited item is first.

**Typical use cases**
- Most popular dashboards within a group: `filter=hash ~= "group/123/dashboard"&groupBy=hash&limit=10`
- Most popular dashboards within a device: `filter=hash ~= "device/456/dashboard"&groupBy=hash&limit=10`
- Page-type popularity (collapse specific IDs into patterns): `groupBy=hash&pathMask=true`
- Most active accounts: `groupBy=accountId&limit=5`

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
filtrex filter expression to scope page-view records before aggregation. Strings must be double-quoted. Use `~=` for regex matching.

**Examples**
- `hash ~= "group/123/dashboard"` — dashboards inside group 123
- `hash ~= "device/456/dashboard"` — dashboards inside device 456
- `accountId == "acc-001"` — views from a specific account

parameters.name: groupBy
parameters.description: Page-view field to aggregate by. Defaults to `hash`. Valid values: id, identifyId, sessionId, date, scheme, host, path, queryString, hash, remoteHost, referrer, pageTitle, propertyKey, eventType, userType, accountId.

parameters.name: pathMask
parameters.description: When set to `"true"`, all numeric segments in the grouped value are replaced with `*` before counting. This collapses individual IDs into page-type patterns. Example: `#/group/123/dashboard/7` → `#/group/*/dashboard/*`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return. Applies after sorting, so you always get the top-N by count. Omit to return all groups.

200.description: Aggregated page-view counts sorted by count descending.

get.description: Supports optional server-side filtering via filtrex (https://github.com/cshaa/filtrex) and field projection. All string values in filter expressions must be double-quoted.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End of the time range (ISO 8601 string or epoch milliseconds).

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Tenant identifier used to scope the cache query.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
filtrex filter expression applied to mapped session event objects. Strings must be double-quoted.

**Examples**
- `userType == "USER"` — regular authenticated users only
- `userType != "VISITOR"` — exclude anonymous visitors
- `userType in ("USER", "LEAD")` — users and leads
- `accountId == "acc-001"` — sessions for a specific account
- `eventType == "SESSION_STARTED"` — session start events only

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description:
Comma-separated session event fields to return. Valid values: id, identifyId, sessionId, accountId, date, propertyKey, eventType, remoteHost, location, userType, globalContext. Omit to return all fields.

**Examples**
- `id,identifyId,accountId,date` — minimal identity and timestamp
- `id,userType,location,date` — geographic and user type analysis
- `id,identifyId,sessionId,userType` — session tracking with user type

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Field and optional direction to sort results by, in the format `field` or `field:asc` / `field:desc`. Direction defaults to `asc` when omitted. Valid field values match those listed in `fields`.

**Examples**
- `date:desc` — most recent sessions first
- `date:asc` — oldest sessions first
- `accountId:asc` — alphabetical by account
- `userType:asc` — alphabetical by user type

200.description: Filtered and projected session event objects.

get.summary: Returns a concise LLM-readable summary of the API in plain text format.
Returns the full LLM-readable API reference in plain text format.

info.title: Gainsight Sync Microservice API
info.description:
A NestJS microservice that periodically pulls Gainsight PX telemetry (custom events, session events, page views, active-user metrics) from configured tenant domains, caches the data in memory per tenant using a date-sorted array with binary-search range queries, and exposes REST endpoints consumed by dashboards such as Grafana.

All data endpoints require `Authorization: Basic <base64(user:password)>` and a `tenantId` query parameter. Public endpoints: `/health`, `/lastRun`, `/llms.txt`, `/llms-full.txt`.

Interactive docs: https://gainsight.eu-latest.cumulocity.com/apps/gainsight-c8y-openapi/index.html
